channel.title: G. Sicuro on Gabriele Sicuro
channel.description: Recent content in G. Sicuro on Gabriele Sicuro

item.title: Machine learning
item.description: Mean-field models for machine learning # TBA

item.title: Matching problems
item.description: Random matching problems # A to be written summary of results on matching problems in various flavours Mean field models # Random Euclidean matching problems # Inference of matchings #

item.title: Former and current students
item.description: PhD students # From 2021, Urte Adomaityte. King’s College London. From 2025, Rodrigo Emilio Perez Ortiz. Alma Mater Università di Bologna. Master students # 2016, Matteo P. D’Achille. Thesis: On two assignment problems, co-supervised with Sergio Caracciolo, University of Milan. 2018, Gianmarco Perrupato. Thesis: Study of matching on the Bethe lattice, co-supervised with Giorgio Parisi, Sapienza University of Rome. 2021, Anshul Toshniwal. Thesis: The planted multi-index matching problem, co-supervised with Lenka Zdeborová, EPFL.

item.title: The RSB40 Book
item.description: The Project # The far-reaching implications of the theory of spin glasses were highlighted further by the 2021 Nobel Prize in Physics, of which half was awarded to Giorgio Parisi for “the discovery of the interplay of disorder and fluctuations in physical systems from atomic to planetary scales.” Since the first observation in the 1960s, the theory of spin glasses has unexpectedly found applications in a wide array of scientific problems.

item.title: Meccanica Razionale
item.description: Modulo per il corso di laurea triennale in Matematica

item.title: Meccanica razionale per l'Ingegneria
